title: Validate whether hitter/pitcher YTD blend beats a bounded window or no-blend baseline
description:
Two independent audit agents (fantasy-baseball-model-auditor and fantasy-baseball-strategist, 2026-07-10) independently flagged the same risk: internal/projections/blended.go and pitcher_blended.go blend the base FanGraphs RoS projection (depthcharts-ros, which already incorporates 2026 YTD performance with proper regression) with RAW, unregressed season-to-date FP/G. At ~90 GP for a regular, seasonWeight=approxPA/(approxPA+250) is already ~58%. This risks double-counting the same in-season signal and over-reacting to hot/cold streaks.

I extended cmd/backtest.go's --recency-experiment harness (previously ytd/w14/w30/decay21 only) with a 'base' variant (no blend at all, pure depthcharts-ros) to isolate the effect, and ran it live:

  --matchup (last completed week, 2026-06-29..2026-07-05, 7 days):
    base    realized=387.0  meanGap=-19.14
    ytd     realized=405.0  meanGap=-16.57  (current production strategy — WON this week)
    w14     realized=340.0  meanGap=-25.86
    w30     realized=396.0  meanGap=-17.86
    decay21 realized=389.0  meanGap=-18.86

  --weeks 4 (2026-06-15..2026-07-09, 25 days):
    base    realized=1252.0 meanGap=-23.76
    ytd     realized=1226.0 meanGap=-24.80  (current production strategy — worse than base AND w30)
    w14     realized=1151.0 meanGap=-27.80  (clearly worst)
    w30     realized=1261.0 meanGap=-23.40  (best of all 5 variants)
    decay21 realized=1217.0 meanGap=-25.16

Over the longer window, current production (ytd/season-to-date) is beaten by both a bounded 30-day window AND a no-blend baseline. The single-week result flips the other way, consistent with a real-but-modest, sample-dependent effect rather than a huge bug. 25 hitter-days is still a small sample for a strategy decision that affects every lineup every day.
acceptance_criteria: Run --recency-experiment over a much longer window (full season to date, or --weeks 8+) to get a stable read. If w30 (or base) continues to beat ytd, either (a) switch BlendedSource's recent-stats input from unbounded season-to-date to a bounded rolling window, or (b) blend against a preseason (independent) projection instead of an in-season-updated RoS system, or (c) lower the recent-weight ceiling. Extend the same experiment to pitchers (pitcher_blended.go) — the current harness is hitters-only. Update CLAUDE.md's Blended scoring section once a decision is made.
notes: Tooling change (the 'base' variant + HasProjDiag n/a-vs-0.00 fix in internal/backtest/strategy_replay.go and cmd/backtest.go) already committed. This issue is about acting on what the tool shows, not about the tool itself.

title: Remove GS_MAX/GS_MIN static env vars; source everything live from Fantrax
description: Now that GS budget/gscheck fetch real limits live from Fantrax (rosterbot-r10), the GS_MAX/GS_MIN numeric env vars only ever served as a fallback value + feature toggle. Replace with a single GS_TRACKING_ENABLED boolean toggle; on live-fetch failure, send a Pushover alert (PUSHOVER_USER_KEY) instead of falling back to a stale static number. Also remove GS_MAX/GS_MIN from infra/infra.go's ECS secrets and the AWS SSM parameters. Discovered along the way: internal/lineupapi (used by the Lambda in lambda/) transitively imports internal/fantrax, and lambda/go.mod had no replace pin for the go-fantrax fork branch, so cdk synth was already broken on main since PR #29 merged — fixed as part of this work.
notes: PR: https://github.com/nixon-commits/rosterbot/pull/35 (branch worktree-gs-tracking-toggle). Also fixes an unrelated but blocking cdk-deploy failure (lambda/go.mod missing go-fantrax fork pin) that's been broken since PR #29 merged ~9am.
close_reason: GS_TRACKING_ENABLED toggle deployed and verified healthy in production (25 runs, 0 failures since deploy). Fixed unrelated blocking cdk-deploy bug (lambda/go.mod). GS_MAX/GS_MIN SSM parameters deleted, confirmed no remaining references anywhere in the codebase.

title: Fetch live Fantrax GS position limits instead of static GS_MAX/GS_MIN
description: Static GS_MAX/GS_MIN env vars diverge from Fantrax's real per-period limits whenever a scoring period spans more than one calendar week (confirmed: period 1 real 17/21, period 16 real 15/19, both vs configured 10/12). Add go-fantrax GetTeamRosterPositionCounts endpoint, wire internal/fantrax.GetGSLimits, update cmd/optimize.go and internal/gscheck to use it with env-var fallback. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-06-fantrax-gs-limits-design.md.
close_reason: Live Fantrax GS limits wired into optimize + gscheck with env-var fallback; verified directly against the live league for periods 1/15/16/17 (16=15/19, the All-Star break value). go-fantrax PR: https://github.com/pmurley/go-fantrax/pull/7. Follow-up rosterbot-6ds tracks post-merge go.mod cleanup.

title: Team-abbreviation normalization inconsistently applied between projection and schedule paths
description: internal/projections normalizes the Fantrax team via NormalizeTeam before using it as a lookup key (projKey), but the schedule/probable-starter path compares the RAW p.MLBTeam field against normalized map keys (cmd/optimize.go:527,558; internal/optimizer/lineup.go:260). Verified live on 2026-07-10 that Fantrax currently returns canonical abbreviations for all 30 teams (including the recent OAK->ATH rename) matching NormalizeTeam's output, so this is NOT currently causing incorrect behavior. But it's a latent coupling: if Fantrax or MLB statsapi ever diverges on an abbreviation again (exactly the class of change OAK->ATH already was), the schedule-matching path would silently mark that team as 'no game' and bench every one of its hitters, with no error or warning surfaced anywhere.
acceptance_criteria: Normalize once at construction time in internal/fantrax/client.go, at the ~3 sites that set MLBTeam from MLBTeamShortName/TeamShortName, so every downstream consumer (projection AND schedule paths) sees the same canonical form. NormalizeTeam is already idempotent so this is safe to apply at the earliest point without touching the projection path's existing correctness.
notes: From fantasy-baseball-strategist review, 2026-07-10 (finding #4). Defensive hardening, not an active bug.

title: GS budget gate ceil-rounds fractional future SP demand, can over-suppress today's certain starts
description: internal/optimizer/gs_budget.go:164, applyGSGate: estCount := int(math.Ceil(futureEstimated)) turns a fractional rotation-math estimate for a future day without announced probables (e.g. 0.2 expected starts) into a full placeholder entry valued at the roster-wide mean SP projection, competing at FULL value against today's confirmed starters in the value-ranked gate. Combined with valuing every estimated future start at the roster-SP mean (not discounted for its lower certainty), this overstates future claims on the remaining weekly GS budget and can bench a certain, real start today in favor of a future start that may never materialize (probables not yet announced, rainout, roster move, etc). Only fires when GS_TRACKING_ENABLED and the weekly budget is genuinely tight, so scoped, but real when it does.
acceptance_criteria: Value estimated future starts at their fractional expectation rather than ceil-rounding to full placeholder entries (e.g. weight the placeholder's contribution to the ranking by the fractional amount rather than creating a full extra competing entry), and/or add a small certainty premium to today's confirmed starts so near-ties resolve toward the sure thing. The existing exact-tie 'prefer today' rule (gs_budget.go:185-189) already establishes the right intuition — extend it to near-ties, not just exact ones.
notes: From fantasy-baseball-strategist review, 2026-07-10 (finding #2). CLAUDE.md's GS-budget section has been corrected in this same session to describe the actual value-ranked gate (it previously described a since-superseded proportional-split formula).

title: Non-starter SP discount (0.10x) likely too generous for future-date/--matchup planning
description: internal/optimizer/pitcher_lineup.go:67 values a non-probable SP whose team plays at 10% of a full start. A projected-20-pt starter then shows as 2.0 pts, which can exceed a real RP's full projection and slot the SP (who most likely won't actually pitch that day) over an RP who will. Impact is muted for the daily production run (real probables are usually known, so real starters get full value), but it distorts --matchup/future-date planning where probables aren't yet announced. This is a repeat finding — flagged in a March 2026 audit and still unresolved.
acceptance_criteria: Lower the non-starter discount from 0.10x to something in the 0.03-0.05x range, or make it a function of how many days out the projection is (probables are announced ~1-5 days ahead for most teams; days further out should discount harder). Verify against backtest --matchup replays that the change doesn't regress lineups where the discount currently happens to work out.
notes: From fantasy-baseball-strategist review, 2026-07-10 (finding #3), confirmed unchanged since a March 2026 audit.

title: ATC RoS FanGraphs projections (atc-ros) return zero rows for both bat+pit — confirmed upstream 500, but pipeline has no visibility into it
description:
internal/projections.LoadPitcherProjections("atc-ros",...) and LoadBattingProjections("atc-ros",...) both return NoData=true / Len()=0 (confirmed live via a direct uncached package call, 2026-07-10). This is NOT a rosterbot bug: I loaded https://www.fangraphs.com/projections?type=ratc&stats=pit... and stats=bat... in a real browser and FanGraphs' OWN frontend makes the identical request (GET /api/projections?type=ratc&stats=bat|pit...) and gets a 500 from their own server for both. Our fgProjectionType mapping ('ratc') is correct; FanGraphs' backend is currently broken for the ATC RoS type (both roles), for reasons outside our control. steamer-ros, depthcharts-ros, and thebatx-ros all load fine (verified same test: Len 4667-5854 bat / 723-5854 pit range).

The real, fixable gap: the shadow/grade/report pipeline (internal/report's rankSystems, cmd/shadow.go's per-system capture) has no visibility into a system coming back with LoadResult.NoData=true. LoadPitcherProjections/LoadBattingProjections silently return an empty stub 'so the optimizer can still run' (correct for the live optimize path, which only ever uses one configured system) but shadow explicitly loops all 4 shadowSystems including atc-ros with no fallback (LoadPitcherProjections only chains RoS->preseason->CSV for a BASE system name; an explicit '-ros' input skips straight to a single API attempt then CSV). Per the model-auditor's independent M2 finding: report.rankSystems has no common-support/minimum-N guard, so a system silently grading projected=0 for every player can be misread as 'ATC is inaccurate' rather than 'ATC had no data that day' — actively misleading on the projection-site dashboard, and this has likely been happening silently every day since the outage started (Shadow's ECS task exits 0 regardless, since NoData isn't an error).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Re-check the FanGraphs endpoint periodically — this piece may self-resolve. 2) Add NoData visibility: either skip writing a shadow snapshot for a system+role that came back NoData (rather than writing a zero/empty one), or tag SnapshotPlayer with a HasProjection flag so report.rankSystems can exclude no-data systems from ranking instead of silently grading them as projected=0. 3) Surface a warning (log line or Pushover) when shadow's capture hits NoData for any of the 4 systems, so a future upstream outage on any system doesn't go unnoticed.
notes: Overlaps with fantasy-baseball-model-auditor's M2 finding (system-comparison ranking conflates coverage with accuracy) from the 2026-07-10 review — this issue supplies the confirmed concrete instance (atc-ros, both roles, upstream 500) plus root cause.

title: Harden optimize.go's period-number resolution to match gscheck's derivation
description:
Final whole-branch review of rosterbot-r10 (live Fantrax GS limits) found that cmd/optimize.go resolves the scoring-period number via GetMatchupWeekNumberForDate (a positional count over matchupWeekRanges' same-opponent date groupings), while internal/gscheck.RunGSCheck uses period.Number (parsed directly from Fantrax's own 'Scoring Period N' caption text). These are structurally independent derivations with no code-level guarantee of agreement -- matchupWeekRanges groups purely by consecutive same-opponent entries with no gap check, so a genuine back-to-back rematch against the same opponent would desync the position count from the real ScoringPeriod number, silently (GetGSLimits would still return valid data for the wrong period, no error/warning).

Verified directly against the live 2026 league (all 23 scoring periods this season, via GetScoringPeriodsAndTeams + GetMatchupWeekNumberForDate cross-check): zero mismatches, including period 16 (the All-Star break this whole effort was built for). So this does NOT manifest as a live bug this season -- filing as a hardening fast-follow, not a regression.

Suggested fix: source the period number in cmd/optimize.go's GS-budget block the same structurally-guaranteed way gscheck.go does -- via GetAllMatchupEntries()'s ScoringPeriod field (or an equivalent lookup keyed off today's date) instead of GetMatchupWeekNumberForDate's positional count. Before landing, verify MatchupEntry.ScoringPeriod is uniform across all entries within a single merged week (e.g. period 16's Jul13-26 span) -- not yet confirmed.

title: No-projection blend fallback trusts raw 2-GP YTD at 100% weight
description: internal/projections/blended.go:59-65 and pitcher_blended.go:66-72: when a player has no FanGraphs projection but has recent Fantrax stats, the blend returns recent.FPtsPerGame UNSHRUNK at 100% weight, gated only by GamesPlayed >= minGP (BLEND_MIN_GP, default 2). The shrinkage formula (seasonWeight = approxPA/(approxPA+250)) only applies on the path where a base projection also exists. So a just-called-up player who homers twice in 2 games gets projected as a season-long standout; scoreRoster (internal/optimizer/lineup.go:265-268) feeds that straight into the optimizer and the snapshot. Cold 2-game players are correctly benched near-zero, but hot 2-game players can be started over proven options — asymmetric risk concentrated exactly in the fringe/call-up population that waiver and streaming decisions hinge on.
acceptance_criteria: Regress the standalone recent value toward a positional/league-average baseline using the same n/(n+k) shrinkage idea (prior = league-avg FP/G at that position) instead of trusting it at 100%, or raise the projection-less minGP threshold to ~10-15 GP before treating raw YTD as a full projection.

title: Repoint go-fantrax replace at pmurley/go-fantrax once GAMES_PER_POS PR merges
description: rosterbot-r10 temporarily points the go-fantrax replace directive at the nixon-commits/go-fantrax add-games-per-position-endpoint branch (see go.mod). Once https://github.com/pmurley/go-fantrax/pull/7 merges to pmurley/go-fantrax main, run: go mod edit -replace github.com/pmurley/go-fantrax=github.com/nixon-commits/go-fantrax@main (or drop the replace and bump the require version if depending on pmurley/go-fantrax directly is preferred), then go mod tidy, go build ./..., go test ./..., commit.

title: Move run ledger to its own S3 prefix (runledger/) instead of sharing runs/
description:
Follow-up to the /v1/runs pagination fix (PR #27, internal/lineupapi/s3lineup/runs.go). Root cause of that bug: the run ledger (runs/<invts>-<id>.json, listed by RunsStore.recent()) shares the runs/ S3 prefix with per-run output blobs (runs/<hex-id>/output.json, written by OutputStore). Lexicographic key ordering interleaves the two, so listing the ledger requires paginating past an ever-growing pool of unrelated output keys.

PR #27 fixed this on the reader side (recent() now follows NextContinuationToken instead of assuming a single page). That's correct and already deployed, but every /v1/runs and /v1/runs/{id} call still pages through however many output sub-keys happen to sort before the ledger records it needs - cost grows with total run count, not with `limit`.

This ticket is to actually separate the two into distinct prefixes so ledger listing goes back to being a cheap, bounded operation with no dependency on how many output blobs exist.
design:
- Add a new prefix (e.g. runledger/) for ledger records only; leave OutputStore on runs/<id>/output.json (output blobs are looked up by exact key via Get(), never listed, so they don't need to move).
- Update both call sites that construct RunsStore with s3lineup.NewRuns(ctx, bucket, "runs/"):
  - cmd/ledger.go:79 (the run-ledger CLI writer, PutRun)
  - lambda/main.go:42 (the read path, List/Get)
  Change both to "runledger/".
- One-time migration: copy existing runs/<invts>-<id>.json objects (NOT the runs/<hex-id>/output.json sub-objects) to runledger/<invts>-<id>.json in STATE_BUCKET. Can be a small one-off Go script or `aws s3api list-objects-v2` + filtered `aws s3 cp` loop (must exclude keys containing "/" after the runs/ prefix, matching the existing flat-key filter logic in RunsStore.recent()).
- After migration is verified (both old runs/*.json ledger keys and new runledger/*.json exist and /v1/runs still reports correctly against the new prefix), old runs/*.json ledger records can be left in place (harmless, no longer listed) or cleaned up later - not urgent since they're small flat JSON objects.
- Once runledger/ is confirmed to contain only flat ledger keys, recent() no longer strictly needs the "/" sub-key filter or even the pagination loop for correctness (only for scaling past 1000 total ledger records) - but there's no harm in leaving both, they're now just defensive rather than load-bearing.
- Verify old and new list against each other during cutover (e.g. run both prefixes' List side by side for a day) before removing the "runs/" writer path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Ledger writer (cmd/ledger.go) and reader (lambda/main.go) both point at runledger/ prefix
- Existing ledger records migrated from runs/*.json to runledger/*.json with no data loss (spot-check record count and a handful of IDs match)
- GET /v1/runs and /v1/runs/{id} verified live against the new prefix, newest-first, matching pre-migration output
- go test ./... passes with updated s3lineup tests reflecting the new prefix
close_reason: Shipped in #28 (merged aec08ed). Writer (cmd/ledger.go) and reader (lambda/main.go) both on runledger/; infra IAM updated; 409 ledger records migrated from runs/ to runledger/ (verified: independent count match 409=409, byte-identical spot check); GET /v1/runs and /v1/runs/{id} verified live post-deploy, newest-first. Follow-up rosterbot-q3b filed for optional cleanup of the now-orphaned old runs/*.json ledger keys.

title: CYC/NH/PG bonus stats unhandled in MLB game-log backfill scorer
description: CYC (cycle) is a declared league scoring category but is absent from ApplyHitter's statMap (internal/scoring/hitter.go:36-42); NH (no-hitter) and PG (perfect game, listed at 25/75 pts in strategist's review) are similarly absent from ApplyPitcher. Correctly omitted from projections (unprojectable event-level bonuses), and self-cancels in grading since both projected and actual sides omit them equally. The only real gap is internal/fantrax/mlb_backfill.go's single-game log scorer (used by recap/backtest for a waiver pickup's first-day FPts), which COULD detect these from one game's box line (cycle = >=1 each of 1B/2B/3B/HR in a game; no-hitter/perfect game from H/BB/HBP/ER over 9 IP) but currently doesn't, so a backfilled day containing one of these events under-counts FPts by the bonus amount.
acceptance_criteria: Add cycle/no-hitter/perfect-game detection to the mlb_backfill.go single-game scorer so historical grading is exact. Confirm the league's actual Fantrax CYC/NH/PG weights first (bd notes both agents flagged this independently but neither confirmed the live weight value).
notes: From both fantasy-baseball-model-auditor (L1) and fantasy-baseball-strategist (finding #5) reviews, 2026-07-10. Very low real-world frequency (~2-3 cycles/year leaguewide); lowest priority of this review's findings.

title: Delete stale runs/*.json ledger records after runledger/ migration
description: rosterbot-432 migrated 409 run-ledger records from runs/*.json to runledger/*.json (both writer and reader now point at runledger/, verified live). The old runs/*.json ledger objects were deliberately left in place per the migration design (harmless, no longer listed by RunsStore -- only runs/<id>/output.json sub-keys are still read there). This is optional cleanup to reclaim the ~409 stale flat objects once nobody needs to cross-reference the old location; not urgent.
acceptance_criteria: Old flat runs/<invts>-<id>.json ledger keys (as opposed to runs/<id>/output.json sub-keys, which must be preserved) are deleted from the live STATE_BUCKET; runs/<id>/output.json objects and GET /v1/runs are unaffected.
